eventMetadata.context_description: Emmanuel Macron has repeatedly affirmed his commitment to completing his second presidential term through May 2027, despite persistent parliamentary instability that has produced multiple short-lived prime ministers and repeated no-confidence challenges since the 2024 legislative elections. Recent statements, including an April 2026 confirmation that he will exit politics entirely after the term, reinforce constitutional term limits and reduce speculation about early departure. Ongoing budget disputes and fragmented opposition in the National Assembly continue to pressure the executive but have not altered his public stance against resignation or snap presidential elections. Traders assessing the timeline therefore weigh these repeated pledges against the risk of escalating crises that could force institutional changes before the scheduled handover.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the Labour Party's heavy losses in the May 2026 local elections across England, Scotland, and Wales, where Reform UK made major gains and national polling showed Reform ahead at around 26 percent, traders are assessing whether Prime Minister Keir Starmer will face mounting pressure to call an early general election before the scheduled deadline of August 2029. Recent by-elections and shifting voter sentiment toward smaller parties have amplified speculation about parliamentary instability, yet the government has shown no signs of dissolving Parliament soon amid ongoing policy challenges like economic growth and public service reforms. Upcoming events such as further by-elections and national opinion polls could influence timing, but historical precedent favors full-term governments unless a no-confidence vote or internal crisis intervenes.

title: China x India military clash by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of a military clash between China and India occurring by December 31.

markets.question: China x India military clash by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of China (People's Republic of China) and India between January 29, and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between Chinese and Indian military forces. Hand-to-hand combat with the use of melee weapons between military personnel, will qualify. For example the 2020 Galwan Valley clash and the 2022 Yangtse clash would both count.

Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the China Coast Guard (CCG) is part of the military, however the Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is not.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both countries continue high-altitude troop deployments along the disputed Line of Actual Control following the 2020 Galwan clash, even as they implemented 2024 patrolling arrangements and completed phased disengagements in key Ladakh sectors. Diplomatic channels remain active, with October 2025 military talks reaffirming use of existing mechanisms to address ground-level issues, alongside resumed direct flights and expanded trade discussions in 2025. India has added forward airbases and infrastructure near the frontier, while China maintains core territorial claims including Arunachal Pradesh. These steps have produced a period of relative stability without reported major incidents since 2024, though the absence of a final boundary settlement and persistent forward postures keep the risk of localized patrol frictions elevated through mid-2026.

title: NATO/EU troops fighting in Ukraine by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if active military personnel officially affiliated with any NATO or EU country enter Ukraine for combat-related military purposes directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, they must be 1) officially acknowledged as active military by the NATO or EU entity or member state they are affiliated with; 2) be publicly acknowledged by NATO or an EU-affiliated entity to have entered Ukraine for a combat-related military purpose directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia.

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution they need be active duty and acknowledged as described above. Participation in a combat role is necessary for this market to resolve to "Yes" (e.g. military personnel providing training or intelligence support would not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, however drone pilots or infantry directly attacking Russian troops, or soldiers targeting and downing missiles from Ukrainian soil would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Western governments continue to prioritize indirect military assistance to Ukraine through weapons deliveries, training programs, and financial aid while avoiding direct combat deployments that could trigger broader NATO-Russia confrontation. Recent NATO and EU discussions have centered on potential post-ceasefire reassurance forces, yet Russian objections and alliance concerns over escalation have kept such plans off the table. In May 2026, focus remains on coordinated support mechanisms and European defense industry scaling rather than troop commitments inside Ukrainian territory. Upcoming alliance summits and bilateral security talks may clarify any shifts, though historical patterns show NATO members favoring non-combat roles to maintain deterrence without crossing into active fighting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Keir Starmer faces mounting internal pressure within the Labour Party following heavy losses in the May 2026 local and devolved elections, which have triggered resignations from cabinet members and aides along with calls from more than 95 MPs for his resignation or a set departure timetable. Recent polling shows his net favourability at -46, with Reform UK leading voting intentions and Labour support fragmented. Starmer has publicly pledged to contest any leadership challenge and defend his position, while potential successors such as Andy Burnham and Wes Streeting position themselves amid ongoing party divisions. Traders are weighing the speed of any formal contest against the prime minister’s ability to stabilize support ahead of the next scheduled general election.

title: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement. 

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995, “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia, which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement, is an example of a qualifying agreement.

An official unilateral pledge by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution even if not part of an agreement with Russia.

The April 19, 2013, Brussels Agreement between Serbia and Kosovo, in which Serbia recognized Kosovo’s de facto administration but did not formally grant de jure recognition, would not be considered a qualifying agreement under this market, as this market requires formal recognition of sovereignty rather than acknowledgment of administrative control.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russia maintains its core demand that Ukraine formally recognize Russian sovereignty over annexed regions including Crimea and parts of Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia, and Kherson as a precondition for any ceasefire or broader agreement. In mid-May 2026, Russian officials reiterated this position while Ukrainian forces conducted major drone strikes on oil infrastructure and defense plants near Moscow, underscoring ongoing military pressure. markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials and prosecutorial setbacks in Harvey Weinstein’s repeated New York retrials have solidified trader consensus around minimal additional prison time. The latest deadlock on May 15, 2026, in the third proceeding on charges tied to Jessica Mann capped a pattern of mixed verdicts and hung juries since the original 2020 conviction was overturned. Weinstein, now 74 and managing documented health issues, continues serving his separate 16-year California sentence, which remains under appeal and runs consecutively. Industry observers note how accumulated time served, sentencing credits, and the Manhattan DA’s hesitation over a potential fourth trial have shifted momentum away from extended incarceration, with appeals court rulings and retrial decisions as the next key catalysts.

title: Will OpenAI launch a consumer hardware product by...?
description: This is a market on whether OpenAI will release a consumer hardware product by the specified date.

markets.question: Will OpenAI launch a new consumer hardware product in 2025?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI publicly announces and launches a new consumer hardware product by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

A "consumer hardware product" is defined as a physical device intended for direct use by individual consumers, rather than enterprise or developer tools. The product must be newly introduced and not a rebrand, update, or iteration of any previously released device.

Examples that would qualify include an AI-powered wearable, smart home assistant, or augmented reality device. Developer tools, AI chips, or servers marketed exclusively for enterprise use would not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

Recent statements from executives confirm the company is on track for a second-half 2026 reveal of an initial AI-native device, likely a screenless smart speaker with camera integration, following prototype development and manufacturing partnerships. This timeline positions OpenAI against established players like Apple in the emerging AI hardware space, where voice-first and context-aware large language model interactions could reshape user adoption. Key upcoming catalysts include any late-2026 announcements or developer updates that could signal production readiness, though supply chain scaling and regulatory factors around data privacy remain potential hurdles for meeting aggressive targets.

title: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of Russia capturing Kostyantynivka by a specific date.

markets.question: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by August 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by August 31, 2025 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by August 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka3.jpg

Train Station Location in Kostyantynivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka2.jpg

Kostyantynivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka1.jpg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by April 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka3.jpg

Train Station Location in Kostyantynivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka2.jpg

Kostyantynivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka1.jpg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified infiltration tactics and small-unit assaults along the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka axis in Donetsk Oblast as part of their spring 2026 offensive toward the Donbas fortress belt. Recent geolocated reports confirm limited Russian footholds on the southern and western outskirts with occasional street-level engagements in peripheral districts, while Ukrainian defenders have conducted counterattacks and drone strikes that disrupt Russian mechanized columns and consolidation efforts. These developments occur amid broader Russian attempts to approach Kramatorsk and Sloviansk, constrained by Ukrainian fortifications, manpower distribution, and terrain. Market positioning reflects trader assessments of whether incremental Russian gains can overcome Ukrainian resistance before any resolution deadline, with potential shifts possible from reinforcement reallocations or escalated long-range strikes.

title: Spain snap election called by...?
description:

This market is about whether a date for the next Spanish election is announced within the stated timeframe. The date the election is scheduled to take place on will have no effect on the resolution to this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez has repeatedly stated his intention to complete the full legislative term with his minority government, steering toward the regularly scheduled general election due no later than August 2027 rather than dissolving parliament early. Recent regional setbacks for the PSOE in Andalusia and Aragon, combined with ongoing coalition fragility involving Junts and uncertainty over the 2026 General State Budget, have fueled speculation about an early contest, yet Sánchez has rejected opposition calls for a snap vote following 2025 corruption-related developments. No motion of no confidence has advanced, and the prime minister continues to emphasize stable governance and progressive policy priorities ahead of potential 2027 regional and local ballots. These factors shape trader assessments of the low likelihood of parliamentary dissolution before year-end absent a major crisis.

title: US x Russia military clash by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing Russia-Ukraine hostilities and associated diplomatic channels remain the central driver of trader assessments for any direct U.S.-Russia military encounter. A May 19 statement by Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergei Ryabkov highlighted mounting risks of NATO-Russia confrontation tied to escalated tensions and nuclear rhetoric, though no U.S. combat involvement has occurred. Recent U.S.-brokered ceasefire attempts, including the short-lived May 9-11 pause, collapsed into renewed Russian strikes, sustaining concerns over inadvertent escalation in Ukrainian airspace or naval incidents. Broader U.S. sanctions enforcement actions earlier in the year and ongoing bilateral talks continue to frame relations below the threshold of direct clash. Scheduled diplomatic engagements and battlefield developments through June could still influence near-term probabilities.

title: Will Russia invade a NATO country by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Russia commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of any NATO country between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by NATO member states or Russia as of May 28, 2025, will be considered the sovereign territory of those countries.

Hybrid or “grey zone” operations conducted by unmarked personnel will qualify if they are confirmed to be Russian operatives and are intended to establish de facto control over territory, as occurred in Crimea (2014) or Donbas (2014–2022).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russia's ongoing military campaign in Ukraine has depleted its conventional forces and constrained options for a direct assault on NATO territory, leading analysts to emphasize hybrid tactics such as sabotage, disinformation, and limited gray-zone probes over large-scale invasion. Lithuanian intelligence reports from March 2026 documented Russian unit expansions and new missile brigades along NATO's eastern flank, while assessments from think tanks highlight potential low-level incursions in the Baltics, including areas near the Suwałki Gap or Estonian border zones. NATO members have responded with increased troop rotations, infrastructure hardening, and Article 5 readiness exercises, reducing the near-term likelihood of escalation while maintaining focus on deterrence ahead of any scheduled alliance summits or force posture reviews.

title: Trump eliminates capital gains tax on crypto by ___?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump signs a bill into law or performs any executive action changing US tax law to eliminate capital gains tax on any cryptocurrency assets by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Such a bill must apply to US taxpaying persons in general; if a law applies only to specific companies or institutions, it will not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market.

If capital gains is eliminated only for specific types or classes of crypto assets (e.g., only Bitcoin or Ethereum, only American issued cryptocurrencies, or only NFTs), it will still count toward a "Yes" resolution for this market.

If the bill is signed into law, or executive action is instituted before the resolution date, it will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of when it takes effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent US-mediated security talks between Israel and Syria, including high-level meetings in Paris in early January 2026, established a joint coordination mechanism to reduce border tensions and limit Iranian influence following the 2024 fall of the Assad regime. Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa has pursued diplomatic engagement with Israel under American oversight, while Israeli officials emphasize protection of strategic red lines and minority communities in southern Syria. However, April 2026 statements from al-Sharaa indicated that Israel withdrew from broader normalization discussions at a late stage, amid ongoing Israeli military actions and Syria's challenges consolidating control over armed factions. These developments shape trader views on the feasibility of formal diplomatic relations by target dates, with progress hinging on de-escalation channels and regional stability.

Advances remain incremental and focused on villages tens of kilometers from Sumy city itself, amid ongoing artillery exchanges and drone activity. eventMetadata.context_description: The 2025-26 NHL playoffs have narrowed to the conference finals, where the Carolina Hurricanes and Colorado Avalanche enter as the clear frontrunners for the Stanley Cup based on regular-season dominance and playoff pedigree. Carolina posted 113 points and reached the Eastern final for the third time in four years, facing a young Montreal Canadiens squad that advanced via a dramatic Game 7 overtime victory. In the West, the Presidents' Trophy-winning Avalanche with 121 points hold home-ice advantage against the Vegas Golden Knights, who are riding momentum from their 2023 title and a strong postseason run under new coaching. Trader consensus reflects these structural edges in depth, goaltending stability, and experience while noting the realistic paths for the underdogs to force series shifts.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities around France, Spain, and England reflects ongoing UEFA qualification campaigns where all three sides have posted strong results alongside favorable schedules and minimal major injuries. Spain’s fluid attacking play and depth have kept them within striking distance of France, whose experienced core continues to deliver consistent performances. England’s solid defensive organization and attacking options sustain their position, while Brazil and Portugal maintain relevance through individual brilliance and South American results. This bunching underscores how recent form, squad health, and qualification momentum continue to shape trader consensus in a wide-open field with many paths to the final.
